title: Grid Identity, Coordinates, Geometry, and Units · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent accessibility club receives a different grid, seed, algorithm, input profile, display scale, assistance need, platform, and failure condition and transfers grid identity, coordinates, geometry, and units into a changed typed logical-grid and viewport geometry model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of typed logical-grid and viewport geometry model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-c…
objectives: Represent stable row-column identity separately from storage offset, graph vertex, pixel rectangle, and display label.
Convert between row-column, linear index, logical point, and Canvas coordinates with explicit origins and axis direction.
Name cells, pixels, logical units, scale factors, padding, and line widths in every geometry formula.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Cell Identity
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab puzzle team handles case 4465 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile logical grid identity, viewport geometry, units, and coordinate round trips; all graph claims need an independent witness; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Geometry Properties
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-geometry-properties-12uf5w" that uses case 4993 to reconcile logical grid identity, viewport geometry, units, and coordinate round trips. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Geometry Properties.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Geometry Properties into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Geometry Properties, keep Geometry Properties connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-geometry-properties-12uf5w
# Competency: maze-geometry-properties.
# Case 4993: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
def evidence_maze_geometry_properties_12uf5w(rows: int = 6, columns: int = 8, cell_size: int = 24, mapped_cells: int = 48, round_trips: int = 48) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_12uf5w = "12uf5w-w5fu21-4993"
    assert evidence_variant_12uf5w
    evidence_axes_12uf5w = (
        "grid12uf5w",
        "topology4993",
        "searchw5fu21",
        "event12uf5w4993",
        "access499312uf5w",
        "recoveryw5fu214993",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_12uf5w)) == 6
    if rows < 1 or columns < 1 or cell_size < 1:
        return False, mapped_cells, "geometry-shape"
    if mapped_cells != rows * columns:
        return False, mapped_cells, "cell-identity"
    if round_trips != mapped_cells:
        return False, round_trips, "coordinate-round-trip"
    if rows * columns > 1000000:
        return False, mapped_cells, "grid-budget"
    assert mapped_cells == rows * columns
    return True, mapped_cells, "bounded-grid-geometry"
